div1.entryFree: Meteor, a bright phenomenon, thought to be portentous, appearing in the atmosphere (cf. Exhalation): it shall hang like a m. o'er the cuckold's horns, Wiv. II, 2, 292. his heart's --s tilting in his face, Err. IV, 2, 6. they will pluck away his natural cause and call them --s, prodigies and signs, John III, 4, 157. had I seen the vaulty top of heaven figured quite o'er with burning --s, V, 2, 53. --s fright the fixed stars of heaven, R2 II, 4, 9. like the --s of a troubled heaven, H4A I, 1, 10. do you see these --s? do you behold these exhalations? what think you they portend? II, 4, 351. and be no more an exhaled m., a prodigy of fear, V, 1, 19. it is some m. that the sun exhales, Rom. III, 5, 13. I missed the m. once, H8 V, 4, 52.
